pulse_items.title: Artemis II Crew Returns Safely to Earth
pulse_items.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully concluded with splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. Viral images show astronauts smiling through recovery hatch windows, sparking renewed public excitement about lunar exploration. The crew will undergo 45 days of medical monitoring.

pulse_items.title: Pragmata Pre-Load Crashes Servers
pulse_items.summary: Capcom's highly anticipated Pragmata game pre-load caused massive server overloads across PlayStation Network and Xbox Live. Reddit's gaming community is exploding with memes about error codes and speculation about the game's mysterious narrative.

pulse_items.title: Microsoft Unveils Agentic AI Platform
pulse_items.summary: At Azure Summit, Microsoft announced Project Cortex - an agentic AI system that can autonomously complete complex multi-step tasks. The demonstration showed AI coordinating travel, meetings, and research without human intervention, sparking both excitement and ethical debates.

pulse_items.title: 'Cat Piano' Meme Takes Over TikTok
pulse_items.summary: A bizarre trend where cats appear to play piano when their paws hit keys is going viral. The #CatPiano hashtag has 2.3M videos, with creators using clever editing to make it look like pets are composing symphonies. Save-to-view ratios are unusually high at 15%.

pulse_items.title: Netflix's 'Chrono Heist' Breaks Records
pulse_items.summary: The time-travel heist series premiered today and immediately became Netflix's most-watched show in 92 countries. Social media is flooded with theories about the show's paradoxical plot, while critics praise its innovative non-linear storytelling.

pulse_items.title: MLB's Robotic Umpire System Controversy
pulse_items.summary: Tonight's Yankees-Red Sox game featured multiple controversial calls by the new automated strike zone system. Players, managers, and fans are divided on the technology, with #RobotUmps trending as traditionalists clash with tech advocates.

pulse_items.title: AI-Generated Cooking Recipes Go Wrong
pulse_items.summary: A viral thread shows disastrous results from following AI-generated recipes, including 'chocolate lasagna' and 'sushi smoothies.' The trend highlights both the limitations of current culinary AI and the human desire to experiment with bizarre food combinations.

pulse_items.title: Quantum Internet Test Successful
pulse_items.summary: Researchers at MIT and TU Delft demonstrated the first city-scale quantum internet connection, achieving theoretically unhackable communication. The breakthrough could revolutionize cybersecurity but raises questions about quantum computing's societal impact.

pulse_items.title: 2000s Fashion Comeback on Instagram
pulse_items.summary: Low-rise jeans, butterfly clips, and velour tracksuits are making a major comeback. Fashion influencers are posting 'throwback hauls' while debating whether these trends should stay in the past. The aesthetic shift represents Gen Z's nostalgia for pre-smartphone era style.

pulse_items.title: Discord's 'Ambient Gaming' Movement
pulse_items.summary: Niche gaming communities are obsessed with 'ambient games' - titles focused on atmosphere rather than objectives. Games like 'Cloudscape' and 'Driftwood' are seeing massive Discord server growth as players seek relaxing, non-competitive experiences.

pulse_items.title: Taylor Swift Announces 'Eras Tour' Film Sequel
pulse_items.summary: Swift surprised fans with an announcement that 'Eras Tour: The Encore' will premiere exclusively on Apple TV+ next month. The announcement crashed Ticketmaster's website again as fans hoped for new tour dates instead.

pulse_items.title: Four-Day Work Week Goes Mainstream
pulse_items.summary: LinkedIn data shows 43% of tech companies have adopted four-day work weeks, with productivity metrics showing 22% average improvement. The trend is spreading to traditional industries, sparking debates about work-life balance and economic implications.

pulse_items.title: Mysterious Bioluminescent Algae Bloom
pulse_items.summary: California beaches are experiencing an unprecedented bioluminescent algae bloom, creating stunning blue waves at night. Scientists are investigating whether climate change or pollution triggered the phenomenon, while tourists flock for the natural light show.

pulse_items.title: 'AI Therapist' Ethics Debate Intensifies
pulse_items.summary: The rapid adoption of AI mental health assistants has sparked intense ethical debates. While some praise increased accessibility, psychologists warn about the limitations of algorithmic empathy and data privacy concerns in sensitive therapeutic contexts.

pulse_items.title: Vintage Tamagotchi Trading Frenzy
pulse_items.summary: A surprising nostalgia trend has Gen X and Millennials trading vintage Tamagotchi devices on Facebook Marketplace. Prices for rare 1990s models have tripled in a week, driven by both genuine nostalgia and speculative collecting.
